positions.rationale: Rare combination of 84% EBITDA margins and AI-driven ad growth, but SEC/binary regulatory risk limits it to a smaller barbell weight.
Highest probability-weighted return in the universe driven by IBSRELA's blockbuster commercial ramp; revenue visibility justifies a top position despite biotech volatility.
Dominant OTA trading at a distressed ~13x forward multiple with $6B annual buyback support; temporary demand dislocation creates strong re-rating asymmetry.
Deeply discounted managed-care/PBM hybrid at 8x earnings with best-in-class ROE; flat expected return and strategic transition keep it at a minimum diversifying ballast.
Asset-light ENHANZE royalty engine converting 60% EBITDA margins into aggressive buybacks; near-term cash extraction story warrants a medium weight despite 2027 patent cliff.
Best-in-class exchange and clearing infrastructure trading at a discount to peers; recurring revenue floor and ~$2B annual buyback provide downside support.
Pure-play defense hardware with a record backlog and prime-contractor inflection; high expected return but unresolved cash conversion caps the weight.
Advertising fortress generating 48% FoA margins at ~17x forward; AI capex governance discount creates an attractive base-case grind with buyback support.
Global energy-drink compounder with unrivaled Coca-Cola distribution; included as a low-correlation defensive anchor despite a premium valuation.
Streaming leader transitioning to high-margin monetization at a discounted ~23x forward; ad-tier scaling and $25B buyback support low-double-digit return.
Workflow platform with a $27.7B backlog and durable 20%+ subscription growth; margin recovery narrative after a 40% drawdown supports a core weight.
AI infrastructure backbone with unmatched near-term visibility and 75% gross margins; sized moderately given maturation debates and hyperscaler concentration.
Hypergrowth social platform with 40% EBITDA margins and fortress balance sheet; recent drawdown offers compelling entry if deceleration proves mechanical.
Dominant sleep-therapy franchise enjoying near-monopoly flow-generator share; GLP-1 overhang and 2027 reimbursement risk keep it at a medium weight.
Integrated nuclear and gas IPP levered to data-center load growth; near-term hedge visibility and scarcity pricing support a core position.

notable_rejections.rationale: Hybrid AI silicon/software story with a $73B backlog, but VMware renewal cliff and extreme customer concentration create binary risk that NVDA captures more cleanly.
Unmatched enterprise RPO and AI demand, yet the margin-destructive capex treadmill and 'new normal' capital intensity make it a lower-conviction tech hold than NOW.
Best-in-class CGM franchise, but gross margin stagnation and the legislated 2028 Medicare pricing cliff create a structural ceiling the market underappreciates.
Infrastructure compounder with a growing data-center backlog, yet negative free-cash-flow yield and premium valuation offer insufficient margin of safety.
Elite payments network with durable margins, but core network yield compression and decelerating volume growth limit asymmetry compared to ICE.
Strong HOKA/UGG brands and a net-cash balance sheet, but U.S. volume stall and tariff-driven margin compression make the setup more cyclical than structural.

portfolio.portfolio_thesis: The portfolio is constructed as a barbell of high-conviction growth inflections and idiosyncratic value, deliberately avoiding the most extreme tech-multiple concentration. The core return engine is built on commercial-stage healthcare (ARDX, HALO, RMD), deeply discounted compounders (BKNG, NFLX, META), and AI-exposed cash-flow stories (NOW, NVDA, VST) that span three sectors. A smaller sleeve of defensive, low-correlation names (MNST, CI, ICE) and a tactical barbell bet (APP, RDDT, KTOS) provides diversification across macro factor profiles while keeping every sector well below the 35% concentration limit. By sizing the highest expected-return names—ARDX, RDDT, VST, BKNG—at the top of the book and capping correlated Communication Services and Technology exposure, the book aims to capture asymmetric upside without overloading a single cyclical theme.
key_risks: A synchronized drawdown in AI-exposed names (NVDA, NOW, VST, APP) could pressure performance if hyperscaler capex is cut simultaneously.
Regulatory enforcement or clinical setbacks in healthcare (ARDX, HALO, RMD, CI) may trigger outsized volatility in the portfolio’s largest combined sector sleeve.
The four Communication Services holdings share elevated macro factor similarity, meaning a broad multiple-compression event in internet platforms would disproportionately weigh on returns.
VST’s leveraged balance sheet and 2028 re-hedging cliff expose the utility position to merchant-multiple compression if ERCOT scarcity pricing erodes faster than expected.
KTOS’s rising days-sales-outstanding and negative operating cash flow could force dilutive financing, invalidating the prime-contractor narrative and inflicting sharp drawdowns.
